purpose: Define the exact GO/NO-GO conditions to switch CX.data from demo/mock data to TyA/Cinepolis real-data preview or staging through one controlled connection point.

allowedTargets: one preloaded adapter before modules if explicitly approved
forbiddenTargets: hardcoded Cinepolis data inside UI modules

demo_only.description: Current app/core/data.js declares Mock data layer and generic demo projects.

manifest_preview_only.description: Manifest-only preview can show source-safe project/period readiness, but cannot claim visits/shoppers are live.

sanitized_preview_readonly.description: In-memory or DEV preview from sanitized visits/shoppers/certifications/liquidations. No writes.

dev_runtime_preview.description: Runtime can be switched in DEV only after source-safe input, rollback, smoke plan and explicit GO.

production_runtime.description: Final cutover only after DEV verified, smoke GO, rollback and no demo final source.

requiredBeforeDevRuntimePreview: sanitized visits preview available or manifest-only UI explicitly accepted
Cinepolis represented as normal configurable project
demo data not used as final source
shopper canonical mismatch not silently promoted
certifications already presented preserved or explicitly mapped
liquidations June kept as payment control, not pending visits
requiredBeforeProductionRuntime: runtime shows TyA/Cinepolis real-data preview or staging, not generic demo final source
no Firestore writes unless explicitly authorized
no HR writeback unless Make/outbox gate is active
no payment paid state without audit/evidence
noGoConditions: app/core/data.js still demo as final production source
raw PII in repo or logs
questionnaire_marks imported as independent source while duplicate
JUNIO 26 HN imported automatically despite review_required
certified shoppers asked to repeat preserved certification without rule
runtime switch touches multiple UI modules
claudeRequirements: If UI changes are needed, document for Claude before editing modules.
Claude package must stay small: 3-5 critical tasks.
Claude must keep Cinepolis configurable and not hardcoded.
Claude must keep honest states: demo, preview, staging, imported, production.
academiaRequirements: Manuals must explain HR source configuration.
Academia must explain preview vs staging vs production.
Certification preservation must be reflected in admin training.
Review-required conflicts must have human review guidance.
